rationale: The citing paper uses Ailawadi et al. (2006) in multiple functional places beyond a passing literature list. It cites the paper as part of the retailer-promotion research base in framing prior insights, explicitly invokes its halo-effect argument and finding that most impact is within-category to scope the analysis, and later uses it as an empirical benchmark for whether simulated lift magnitudes are plausible. These uses materially inform the paper's conceptual setup and interpretation even though the model is not directly reused from that specific study.
relation_description: The citing paper treats Ailawadi et al. (2006) as prior retailer-side evidence on promotion effects, especially halo versus within-category impact, to motivate its modeling scope and performance interpretation. It also uses the cited study's reported lift magnitudes as a benchmark for validating simulation outputs.
